---
title: "Yi Xie"
subtitle: "Computational Biologist · single-cell RNA-seq + AI method development"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: profile.jpg
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:yixie97@gmail.com
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=YOUR_ID      # TODO: confirm
    - text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0000-0000-0000                  # TODO: confirm
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/carissaxie
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/YOUR_HANDLE                # TODO: confirm
    - icon: twitter-x
      text: X
      href: https://x.com/YOUR_HANDLE                              # TODO: confirm (or remove)
---

<!-- TODO: confirm bio wording, exact title, and current affiliation. -->

I am a computational biologist (postdoc / research scientist) working at the
intersection of **single-cell RNA-seq analysis** and **AI-based method development**.
My work centers on turning high-dimensional single-cell data into interpretable,
generalizable, and clinically meaningful signals — for example, identifying the cell
populations and compact gene panels that best explain a biological or disease state.

My published tool **[scPanel](projects/index.qmd)** automatically discovers sparse,
generalizable gene panels for patient classification from scRNA-seq data. More broadly,
I build reproducible pipelines and machine-learning methods for **perturbation and
disease-state modeling**, **biomarker / gene-panel discovery**, and **generalizable
single-cell analysis**.

### I'm open to collaborations

- **Biological / wet-lab partners** — if you have single-cell datasets and questions
  (disease mechanisms, drug/perturbation response, biomarker discovery), I'm glad to
  help design and run the computational analysis.
- **Method & tool development** — I enjoy co-developing ML/AI methods for single-cell
  data, from problem framing to a reproducible, well-documented package.

Reach me at [yixie97@gmail.com](mailto:yixie97@gmail.com), or browse my
[research](research.qmd), [projects](projects/index.qmd), and [CV](cv.qmd).
